data.content:
1	---
2	name: blueprint-conventions
3	description: The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
4	---
5	
6	Blueprints are a LaTeX subset (the `leanblueprint` dialect) parsed into the DAG.
7	Write timeless mathematics — a standalone document, not a project journal.
8	
9	## Environments
10	
11	Each declaration is an amsthm-style environment; the recognized ones are
12	`theorem`, `lemma`, `proposition`, `corollary`, `definition`, `remark`,
13	`example`, `notation`, `convention`, and `proof`. A statement environment plus
14	its following `proof` environment form one node's content.
15	
16	## Per-node macros
17	
18	- `\label{id}` — a stable id (the DAG node key).
19	- `\lean{Name}` — the Lean declaration this node formalizes. Keep it 1-to-1: one
20	  blueprint node ↔ one Lean declaration, signatures matching.
21	- `\uses{a,b}` — dependencies on other nodes' labels; these drive the DAG edges.
22	  Add an edge instead of re-explaining a dependency in prose. Put `\uses` in the
23	  statement for definitional deps and in the `proof` for deps used only in the
24	  proof.
25	- `\source{slug:page-0001}` — reference anchors for UI popups and audit trails.
26	  Use page-level ids from `references/<slug>/tex/page-0001.tex` or an equivalent
27	  manifest entry. Multiple anchors may be comma-separated.
28	- `\leanok` — the Lean is written and checked. Honest only when its Lean is
29	  actually checked *and* its dependencies are too. On a `proof`, it means the
30	  proof is formalized; on a statement, the signature is formalized.
31	- `\mathlibok` — the declaration is already available in mathlib (no
32	  formalization needed; treat as a leaf).
33	
34	## House style
35	
36	- **Pure mathematics only.** No Lean tactics, typeclass/implementation notes, or
37	  semi-Lean pseudocode. No project history ("since iter N", "our failed route")
38	  and no conversational filler. If a Lean helper exists with no math counterpart,
39	  write its LaTeX statement so the 1-to-1 correspondence holds.
40	- **Complete proofs, not sketches.** A node's `proof` is a real, rigorous
41	  mathematical proof a reader could check — not a hand-wave or a TODO. The way to
42	  keep it short is to *split*, not to abbreviate: if a proof is long or a node
43	  grows past a short paragraph, break out the hard step as its own `\uses`-linked
44	  lemma (down to sentence-sized lemmas if needed) so each node stays small and
45	  its proof stays complete.
46	- **One declaration's worth per node.** Don't restate Lean source as prose.
47	- **Cite by source link, not quote comments.** Add `\source{...}` to the
48	  relevant node. Keep the retrieved/transcribed page text in `references/` (for
49	  example `references/<slug>/tex/page-0042.tex`) and let the UI expose it; do not
50	  paste verbatim source quotes into `% QUOTE` comments inside the blueprint.
51

data.content:
diff --git a/blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex b/blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex
index 5049541..44b9197 100644
--- a/blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex
+++ b/blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex
@@ -1258,6 +1258,159 @@ either directly or through the Jacobian regular-sequence witness.
   \cref{lem:ringKrullDim_quotient_add_eq_regular_sequence}.
 \end{proof}
 
+\paragraph{Closed-point completion of the 00OE chain (Krull height theorem route).}
+
+The lemmas above reduce the localised Krull-dimension formula at a maximal
+ideal to a regular-sequence witness for the relations of the presentation. The
+following route closes the closed-point case unconditionally, replacing that
+witness by Krull's height theorem: the dimension \emph{lower} bound comes from
+the full height of the pulled-back maximal ideal in the polynomial ring, and
+the \emph{upper} bound is automatic from the cotangent space. Combined with the
+cotangent computation \cref{lem:finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_residue},
+this proves regularity of a standard-smooth algebra at every closed point.
+
+\begin{lemma}\leanok
+[Maximal ideals of polynomial rings over a field have full height]
+  \label{lem:mvPolynomial_height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal}
+  \lean{MvPolynomial.height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal}
+  For a field \(k\) and a finite index set \(I\), every maximal ideal \(M\) of
+  the polynomial ring \(k[X_i : i \in I]\) has height exactly \(\#I\).
+\end{lemma}
+
+\begin{proof}
+  Induction on \(\#I\). For \(I = \emptyset\) the ring is the field \(k\), of
+  Krull dimension \(0\), so every prime has height \(0\). For the induction
+  step write \(k[X_i : i \in I \sqcup \{\ast\}] = R[X]\) with
+  \(R = k[X_i : i \in I]\). Given a maximal ideal \(M \subseteq R[X]\), its
+  contraction \(\mathfrak p = M \cap R\) is again maximal, because \(R\) is a
+  finitely generated algebra over a field and hence a Jacobson ring. For a
+  maximal ideal of \(R[X]\) over a Noetherian ring \(R\) one has
+  \(\operatorname{ht} M = \operatorname{ht} \mathfrak p + 1\): the fibre
+  \(R[X] \otimes_R \kappa(\mathfrak p) = \kappa(\mathfrak p)[X]\) is a
+  principal ideal domain, so the image of \(M\) there has height \(1\), and
+  going-down along the flat extension \(R \to R[X]\) splices the chains. The
+  claim follows from the inductive hypothesis
+  \(\operatorname{ht}\mathfrak p = \#I\).
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{lemma}\leanok
+[Height lower bound at maximal ideals of standard-smooth algebras]
+  \label{lem:standard_smooth_le_height_of_isMaximal}
+  \lean{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.natCast_le_height_of_isMaximal}
+  Let \(k\) be a field and \(S\) a standard-smooth \(k\)-algebra of relative
+  dimension \(n\). Then every maximal ideal \(\mathfrak m \subseteq S\) has
+  height at least \(n\).
+\end{lemma}
+
+\begin{proof}
+  \uses{lem:mvPolynomial_height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal}
+  Choose a submersive presentation \(S = P/I\) with
+  \(P = k[X_1, \dots, X_N]\) and \(I = (f_1, \dots, f_c)\) generated by the
+  \(c\) relations, so that \(n = N - c\); the submersive structure embeds the
+  relation indices into the generator indices, so \(c \le N\) and
+  \(n + c = N\). The preimage \(M \subseteq P\) of \(\mathfrak m\) is a maximal
+  ideal containing \(I\), of height \(N\) by
+  \cref{lem:mvPolynomial_height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal}. Krull's height
+  theorem, in the relative form
+  \(\operatorname{ht} M \le \operatorname{ht}(M/I) + \mu(I)\) where
+  \(\mu(I) \le c\) is the minimal number of generators of \(I\), gives
+  \(N \le \operatorname{ht}(\mathfrak m) + c\) since \(M/I\) corresponds to
+  \(\mathfrak m\) under \(P/I \simeq S\). Hence
+  \(n \le \operatorname{ht}\mathfrak m\).
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{lemma}\leanok
+[Dimension lower bound at closed points of standard-smooth algebras]
+  \label{lem:standard_smooth_le_ringKrullDim_localization}
+  \lean{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.le_ringKrullDim_of_isLocalization_atPrime}
+  Let \(k\) be a field, \(S\) a standard-smooth \(k\)-algebra of relative
+  dimension \(n\), \(\mathfrak m \subseteq S\) a maximal ideal, and
+  \(S_{\mathfrak m}\) a localisation of \(S\) at \(\mathfrak m\). Then
+  \(n \le \dim S_{\mathfrak m}\).
+\end{lemma}
+
+\begin{proof}
+  \uses{lem:standard_smooth_le_height_of_isMaximal,
+        lem:ringKrullDim_localization_eq_height_atPrime}
+  Combine \(\dim S_{\mathfrak m} = \operatorname{ht}\mathfrak m\)
+  (\cref{lem:ringKrullDim_localization_eq_height_atPrime}) with the height
+  lower bound \cref{lem:standard_smooth_le_height_of_isMaximal}.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{lemma}\leanok
+[Regularity from the cotangent upper bound]
+  \label{lem:regular_of_finrank_cotangent_le_dim}
+  \lean{IsRegularLocalRing.of_finrank_cotangentSpace_le_ringKrullDim}
+  Let \(A\) be a Noetherian local ring with maximal ideal \(\mathfrak m\) and
+  residue field \(\kappa\). If
+  \(\dim_\kappa \mathfrak m/\mathfrak m^2 \le \dim A\), then \(A\) is a
+  regular local ring.
+\end{lemma}
+
+\begin{proof}
+  By Nakayama's lemma the minimal number of generators \(\mu(\mathfrak m)\)
+  equals \(\dim_\kappa \mathfrak m/\mathfrak m^2\), and Krull's height theorem
+  gives \(\dim A \le \mu(\mathfrak m)\) in any Noetherian local ring. The
+  hypothesis forces \(\mu(\mathfrak m) = \dim A\), which is the definition of
+  regularity.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{lemma}\leanok
+[Stacks 00TT at a rational closed point, algebra level]
+  \label{lem:standard_smooth_regular_at_rational_point}
+  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.isRegularLocalRing_localization_of_isStandardSmooth_of_bijective_residue}
+  Let \(k\) be a field, \(S\) a standard-smooth \(k\)-algebra,
+  \(\mathfrak m \subseteq S\) a maximal ideal, and \(S_{\mathfrak m}\) a
+  localisation of \(S\) at \(\mathfrak m\) such that the composite
+  \(k \to S_{\mathfrak m} \to \kappa(\mathfrak m)\) is bijective (a
+  \(k\)-rational point). Then \(S_{\mathfrak m}\) is a regular local ring.
+\end{lemma}
+
+\begin{proof}
+  \uses{lem:standard_smooth_le_ringKrullDim_localization,
+        lem:regular_of_finrank_cotangent_le_dim,
+        lem:finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_residue,
+        lem:module_free_kaehler_standardSmooth,
+        lem:module_free_kaehler_localization,
+        lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim,
+        lem:exists_standardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_of_standardSmooth}
+  Let \(n\) be the relative dimension of \(S\) over \(k\)
+  (\cref{lem:exists_standardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_of_standardSmooth}).
+  The ring \(S\) is Noetherian (finite type over a field), hence so is
+  \(S_{\mathfrak m}\), and \(S_{\mathfrak m}\) is formally smooth over \(k\)
+  (a localisation of a smooth algebra). Its K\"ahler module
+  \(\Omega_{S_{\mathfrak m}/k}\) is free of rank \(n\)
+  (\cref{lem:module_free_kaehler_standardSmooth},
+  \cref{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization},
+  \cref{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim}), so by the conormal
+  computation at the \(k\)-rational point
+  (\cref{lem:finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_residue})
+  \(\dim_{\kappa} \mathfrak m/\mathfrak m^2 = n\). Since
+  \(n \le \dim S_{\mathfrak m}\)
+  (\cref{lem:standard_smooth_le_ringKrullDim_localization}), regularity
+  follows from \cref{lem:regular_of_finrank_cotangent_le_dim}.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{lemma}\leanok
+[Stacks 00TT at closed points over an algebraically closed field]
+  \label{lem:standard_smooth_regular_at_closed_point_algclosed}
+  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.isRegularLocalRing_localizationAtPrime_of_isStandardSmooth_of_isAlgClosed}
+  Let \(\bar k\) be an algebraically closed field and \(S\) a standard-smooth
+  \(\bar k\)-algebra. Then for every maximal ideal \(\mathfrak m \subseteq S\)
+  the local ring \(S_{\mathfrak m}\) is regular.
+\end{lemma}
+
+\begin{proof}
+  \uses{lem:standard_smooth_regular_at_rational_point}
+  By Zariski's lemma the residue field \(S/\mathfrak m\) is a finite, hence
+  algebraic, extension of \(\bar k\); as \(\bar k\) is algebraically closed,
+  \(\bar k \to S/\mathfrak m\) is bijective, and composing with the
+  isomorphism \(S/\mathfrak m \simeq \kappa(\mathfrak m)\) (valid since
+  \(\mathfrak m\) is maximal) shows every maximal ideal is a
+  \(\bar k\)-rational point. Conclude with
+  \cref{lem:standard_smooth_regular_at_rational_point}.
+\end{proof}
+
 \paragraph{Stacks 00SW submersive-presentation cotangent independence.}
 
 \begin{lemma}\leanok
@@ -1447,7 +1600,8 @@ either directly or through the Jacobian regular-sequence witness.
         lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula, lem:stage6_regular_stalk_assembly,
         lem:stalkMap_flat_of_smooth, lem:exists_algebra_standardSmooth_stalk_localization,
         lem:finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_residue, lem:gammaSpecField_ringEquiv,
-        lem:exists_standardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_of_standardSmooth}
+        lem:exists_standardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_of_standardSmooth,
+        lem:standard_smooth_regular_at_closed_point_algclosed}
   % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00TT --- Jacobian criterion
   % (smooth-implies-regular direction) (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex,
   % L38593--38611; tag verified against the Stacks website tag/00TT page,

data.content:
1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Krull dimension at maximal ideals of standard-smooth algebras (Stacks 00OE substrate)
10	
11	This file discharges **sub-gap (ii.B)** of the smooth ⟹ regular pipeline of
12	`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` (Stacks tag `00OE`, the smooth-algebra
13	Krull-dimension formula), in the closed-point form the pipeline consumes: for a
14	standard-smooth algebra `S` of relative dimension `n` over a field `k` and a
15	*maximal* ideal `m ⊆ S`, the localisation `Sₘ` has Krull dimension (at least,
16	hence — combined with the cotangent bound — exactly) `n`.
17	
18	Contrary to the iter-199 estimate ("~200–300 LOC on `transcendenceDegree` +
19	Noether normalisation"), no transcendence-degree theory is needed: the v4.31
20	Mathlib toolbox (`Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one`, Krull's height theorem
21	in the form `Ideal.height_le_height_add_spanFinrank_of_le`, and the
22	`MvPolynomial.isJacobsonRing` instance) supports a direct height computation:
23	
24	* `MvPolynomial.height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal`: every maximal ideal of
25	  `MvPolynomial ι k` (finitely many variables over a field) has height
26	  `Nat.card ι`. Induction over `ι` via `Finite.induction_empty_option`; the
27	  induction step contracts a maximal ideal of `R[X]` to a maximal ideal of `R`
28	  (Jacobson) and applies `Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one`.
29	* `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.natCast_le_height_of_isMaximal`:
30	  the **lower bound** `n ≤ ht m` for a maximal ideal `m` of a standard-smooth
31	  `k`-algebra of relative dimension `n`. Writing `S = P.Ring ⧸ ker` for a
32	  submersive presentation `P` with `#ι` generators and `#σ` relations
33	  (`n = #ι - #σ`), the pullback `M ⊆ P.Ring` of `m` is maximal of height `#ι`,
34	  and Krull's height theorem bounds `ht M ≤ ht m + #σ`.
35	* `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.le_ringKrullDim_of_isLocalization_atPrime`:
36	  the same lower bound for `ringKrullDim Sₘ` at the localisation.
37	* `IsRegularLocalRing.of_finrank_cotangentSpace_le_ringKrullDim`: the generic
38	  regularity glue — a Noetherian local ring whose cotangent-space dimension is
39	  bounded by its Krull dimension is regular (the reverse inequality is
40	  automatic).
41	
42	The consumer (`isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` Stage 6 in
43	`CodimOneExtension.lean`) combines the last two lemmas with the iter-199
44	sub-gap (ii.A) helper `finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_algebraMap_residue`
45	(`finrank κ(m) (m/m²) = n` at a `k`-rational closed point) to conclude
46	regularity of the stalk at closed points. The remaining gap for *non-closed*
47	points is Stacks `00OF` (localisations of regular local rings are regular),
48	tracked in `CodimOneExtension.lean`.
49	
50	Blueprint chapter: `blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex`
51	(nodes `lem:mvPolynomial_height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal`,
52	`lem:standard_smooth_le_height_of_isMaximal`,
53	`lem:standard_smooth_le_ringKrullDim_localization`,
54	`lem:regular_of_finrank_cotangent_le_dim`).
55	-/
56	
57	universe u v
58	
59	open Ideal
60	
61	section MvPolynomialHeight
62	
63	/-- **Maximal ideals of finite-variable polynomial rings over a field have full
64	height.** For a field `k`, a finite index type `ι` and a maximal ideal
65	`M ⊆ k[Xᵢ : i ∈ ι]`, the height of `M` is `Nat.card ι`.
66	
67	Induction over `ι` via `Finite.induction_empty_option`: the base case is the
68	field `k` itself (Krull dimension `0`), and the induction step writes
69	`MvPolynomial (Option α) k ≃+* (MvPolynomial α k)[X]`, contracts the maximal
70	ideal along `C` (maximal again, since `MvPolynomial α k` is Jacobson), and
71	applies `Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one`. -/
72	theorem MvPolynomial.height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal
73	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {ι : Type v} [Finite ι]
74	    (M : Ideal (MvPolynomial ι k)) (hM : M.IsMaximal) :
75	    M.height = Nat.card ι := by
76	  induction ι using Finite.induction_empty_option with
77	  | of_equiv e IH =>
78	    haveI := hM
79	    have h1 := IH (M.comap (MvPolynomial.renameEquiv k e).toRingEquiv)
80	      (Ideal.comap_isMaximal_of_equiv _)
81	    rw [RingEquiv.height_comap] at h1
82	    rw [h1, Nat.card_congr e]
83	  | h_empty =>
84	    have hdim : ringKrullDim (MvPolynomial PEmpty.{v + 1} k) = 0 := by
85	      rw [ringKrullDim_eq_of_ringEquiv (MvPolynomial.isEmptyRingEquiv k PEmpty)]
86	      exact ringKrullDim_eq_zero_of_field k
87	    have hle : (M.height : WithBot ℕ∞) ≤ 0 :=
88	      hdim ▸ Ideal.height_le_ringKrullDim_of_ne_top hM.ne_top
89	    have hz : M.height = 0 := le_antisymm (by exact_mod_cast hle) (by simp)
90	    simp [hz]
91	  | h_option IH =>
92	    rename_i α _
93	    haveI := hM
94	    set ψ := (MvPolynomial.optionEquivLeft k α).toRingEquiv with hψ
95	    set P' : Ideal (Polynomial (MvPolynomial α k)) := M.map ψ with hP'
96	    haveI hP'max : P'.IsMaximal := Ideal.map_isMaximal_of_equiv ψ
97	    set p : Ideal (MvPolynomial α k) := P'.comap (Polynomial.C) with hpdef
98	    haveI hpmax : p.IsMaximal := Polynomial.isMaximal_comap_C_of_isJacobsonRing P'
99	    haveI : P'.LiesOver p := ⟨by rw [hpdef, Ideal.under_def, Polynomial.algebraMap_eq]⟩
100	    have hht : P'.height = p.height + 1 := Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one p P'
101	    have hIH : p.height = Nat.card α := IH p hpmax
102	    have hMP' : M.height = P'.height := (RingEquiv.height_map ψ M).symm
103	    have hcard : Nat.card (Option α) = Nat.card α + 1 := by
104	      simp [Nat.card_eq_fintype_card]
105	    rw [hMP', hht, hIH, hcard]
106	    push_cast
107	    ring
108	
109	end MvPolynomialHeight
110	
111	section StandardSmoothLowerBound
112	
113	namespace Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension
114	
115	/-- **Stacks 00OE lower bound (closed-point form).** For a standard-smooth
116	algebra `S` of relative dimension `n` over a field `k` and a maximal ideal
117	`m ⊆ S`, the height of `m` is at least `n`.
118	
119	Proof: extract a submersive presentation `P` (with `#ι` generators, `#σ`
120	relations, `n = #ι - #σ`). The pullback `M` of `m` to the polynomial ring
121	`P.Ring` is maximal of height `#ι`
122	(`MvPolynomial.height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal`), contains the kernel of the
123	presentation (generated by the `#σ` relations), and Krull's height theorem
124	(`Ideal.height_le_height_add_spanFinrank_of_le`) gives
125	`#ι = ht M ≤ ht m + #σ`, whence `n ≤ ht m`. -/
126	theorem natCast_le_height_of_isMaximal
127	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Algebra k S] (n : ℕ)
128	    [H : Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n k S]
129	    (m : Ideal S) (hm : m.IsMaximal) : (n : ℕ∞) ≤ m.height := by
130	  obtain ⟨ι, σ, hσ, hι, P, hPdim⟩ := H.out
131	  haveI := hm
132	  have hsurj : Function.Surjective (algebraMap P.Ring S) := P.algebraMap_surjective
133	  set M : Ideal P.Ring := m.comap (algebraMap P.Ring S) with hMdef
134	  haveI hM : M.IsMaximal := Ideal.comap_isMaximal_of_surjective _ hsurj
135	  -- full height of the pullback in the polynomial ring
136	  have hMheight : M.height = Nat.card ι := MvPolynomial.height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal M hM
137	  -- the presentation kernel sits inside the pullback
138	  have hker_le : RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S) ≤ M := fun x hx => by
139	    rw [hMdef, Ideal.mem_comap, RingHom.mem_ker.mp hx]
140	    exact m.zero_mem
141	  -- Krull's height theorem
142	  have hbound := Ideal.height_le_height_add_spanFinrank_of_le hker_le
143	  -- identify the quotient-side prime with `m` (first isomorphism theorem)
144	  set e := (algebraMap P.Ring S : P.Ring →+* S).quotientKerEquivOfSurjective hsurj with hedef
145	  have hMcomap : M = Ideal.comap (Ideal.Quotient.mk (RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S)))
146	      (Ideal.comap e m) := by
147	    ext x
148	    simp only [hMdef, Ideal.mem_comap]
149	    rw [hedef, RingHom.quotientKerEquivOfSurjective_apply_mk]
150	  have hqheight : (M.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S)))).height
151	      = m.height := by
152	    rw [hMcomap, Ideal.map_comap_of_surjective _ Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective,
153	      RingEquiv.height_comap]
154	  -- the kernel is generated by the `#σ` relations
155	  have hker_span : RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S) = Ideal.span (Set.range P.relation) :=
156	    P.span_range_relation_eq_ker.symm
157	  have hfr : (RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S)).spanFinrank ≤ Nat.card σ := by
158	    rw [hker_span]
159	    refine le_trans (Submodule.spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite (Set.finite_range _)) ?_
160	    calc (Set.range P.relation).ncard
161	        = (P.relation '' Set.univ).ncard := by rw [Set.image_univ]
162	      _ ≤ (Set.univ : Set σ).ncard := Set.ncard_image_le Set.finite_univ
163	      _ = Nat.card σ := Set.ncard_univ σ
164	  -- cardinal bookkeeping: `n + #σ = #ι`
165	  have hcards : n + Nat.card σ = Nat.card ι := by
166	    have hle : Nat.card σ ≤ Nat.card ι := Nat.card_le_card_of_injective P.map P.map_inj
167	    have hdim : Nat.card ι - Nat.card σ = n := hPdim
168	    omega
169	  -- combine
170	  have h1 : (Nat.card ι : ℕ∞) ≤ m.height + Nat.card σ := by
171	    calc (Nat.card ι : ℕ∞) = M.height := hMheight.symm
172	      _ ≤ (M.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S)))).height
173	          + (RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S)).spanFinrank := hbound
174	      _ ≤ m.height + Nat.card σ := by
175	          rw [hqheight]
176	          have hfr' : ((RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S)).spanFinrank : ℕ∞)
177	              ≤ (Nat.card σ : ℕ∞) := by exact_mod_cast hfr
178	          exact add_le_add le_rfl hfr'
179	  rw [← hcards] at h1
180	  push_cast at h1
181	  exact (ENat.add_le_add_iff_right (by simp)).mp h1
182	
183	/-- **Stacks 00OE lower bound, localised form.** For a standard-smooth algebra
184	`S` of relative dimension `n` over a field `k`, a maximal ideal `m ⊆ S` and a
185	localisation `Sₘ` of `S` at `m`, the Krull dimension of `Sₘ` is at least `n`. -/
186	theorem le_ringKrullDim_of_isLocalization_atPrime
187	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Algebra k S] (n : ℕ)
188	    [Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n k S]
189	    (m : Ideal S) (hm : m.IsMaximal)
190	    (Sₘ : Type u) [CommRing Sₘ] [Algebra S Sₘ] [IsLocalization.AtPrime Sₘ m] :
191	    (n : WithBot ℕ∞) ≤ ringKrullDim Sₘ := by
192	  haveI := hm.isPrime
193	  rw [IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height m Sₘ]
194	  exact_mod_cast natCast_le_height_of_isMaximal (k := k) n m hm
195	
196	end Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension
197	
198	end StandardSmoothLowerBound
199	
200	section RegularityGlue
201	
202	/-- **Regularity from the cotangent upper bound.** A Noetherian local ring whose
203	cotangent-space dimension is bounded above by its Krull dimension is a regular
204	local ring (the reverse inequality `dim ≤ dim_κ m/m²` holds in any Noetherian
205	local ring, via `ringKrullDim_le_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal`). This is the glue
206	between the sub-gap (ii.A) cotangent computation and the sub-gap (ii.B)
207	dimension lower bound in the smooth ⟹ regular pipeline. -/
208	theorem IsRegularLocalRing.of_finrank_cotangentSpace_le_ringKrullDim
209	    {A : Type u} [CommRing A] [IsLocalRing A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
210	    (h : (Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField A) (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace A) : WithBot ℕ∞)
211	      ≤ ringKrullDim A) : IsRegularLocalRing A := by
212	  apply IsRegularLocalRing.of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le
213	  rwa [IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace]
214	
215	end RegularityGlue
216

990	
991	/-- **Lane COE Step B.d — Stacks `00TT` at a rational closed point, algebra
992	level (this iter).** For a standard-smooth algebra `S` over a field `k`, a
993	maximal ideal `m ⊆ S` whose induced residue map `k → κ(m)` is bijective (the
994	`k`-rational-point condition, automatic over an algebraically closed field by
995	the Nullstellensatz), the localisation `Sₘ` is a regular local ring.
996	
997	This *closes sub-gap (ii.B) at closed points*: the proof combines
998	* the iter-199 sub-gap (ii.A) cotangent computation
999	  `finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_algebraMap_residue`
1000	  (`finrank κ (m/m²) = n`, from formal smoothness + free Kähler differentials
1001	  of rank `n`), with
1002	* the new `StandardSmoothDimension.lean` dimension lower bound
1003	  `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.le_ringKrullDim_of_isLocalization_atPrime`
1004	  (`n ≤ ringKrullDim Sₘ`, via the polynomial-ring height computation and
1005	  Krull's height theorem — no transcendence-degree theory needed), through
1006	* the generic glue `IsRegularLocalRing.of_finrank_cotangentSpace_le_ringKrullDim`
1007	  (a Noetherian local ring with `dim_κ m/m² ≤ ringKrullDim` is regular).
1008	
1009	The consumer `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` still carries its `sorry`
1010	because its statement quantifies over *all* points `z`: at a non-closed point
1011	the residue field is a transcendental extension of `k̄` and the bijectivity
1012	hypothesis fails; localising this closed-point witness to a general point is
1013	Stacks `00OF` (localisations of regular local rings are regular), the one
1014	remaining genuine Mathlib gap of the pipeline. Axiom-clean. -/
1015	theorem isRegularLocalRing_localization_of_isStandardSmooth_of_bijective_residue
1016	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
1017	    {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Nontrivial S] [Algebra k S]
1018	    [Algebra.IsStandardSmooth k S]
1019	    (m : Ideal S) (hm : m.IsMaximal)
1020	    (Sₘ : Type u) [CommRing Sₘ] [IsLocalRing Sₘ] [Algebra k Sₘ] [Algebra S Sₘ]
1021	    [IsScalarTower k S Sₘ] [IsLocalization.AtPrime Sₘ m]
1022	    (hbij : Function.Bijective (algebraMap k (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ))) :
1023	    IsRegularLocalRing Sₘ := by
1024	  haveI := hm.isPrime
1025	  -- Noetherian structure: standard-smooth ⟹ finite presentation ⟹ Noetherian
1026	  -- over the base field; localisation preserves Noetherianness.
1027	  haveI : IsNoetherianRing S := Algebra.FiniteType.isNoetherianRing k S
1028	  haveI : IsNoetherianRing Sₘ := IsLocalization.isNoetherianRing m.primeCompl Sₘ ‹_›
1029	  -- Extract the relative dimension n.
1030	  obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ :=
1031	    exists_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_of_isStandardSmooth (R := k) (S := S)
1032	  haveI := hn
1033	  -- Kähler package at the localisation (Stages 4, 5a, 5b).
1034	  haveI : Module.Free S (Ω[S⁄k]) := module_free_kaehlerDifferential_of_isStandardSmooth
1035	  haveI : Module.Free Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄k]) :=
1036	    module_free_kaehlerDifferential_localization m.primeCompl Sₘ
1037	  have hrank : Module.rank Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄k]) = n :=
1038	    rank_kaehlerDifferential_localization_eq_relativeDimension n m.primeCompl Sₘ
1039	  -- Formal smoothness of the localisation over k.
1040	  haveI : Algebra.FormallySmooth S Sₘ := Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_isLocalization m.primeCompl
1041	  haveI : Algebra.FormallySmooth k Sₘ := Algebra.FormallySmooth.comp k S Sₘ
1042	  -- (ii.A): cotangent finrank = n at the k-rational closed point.
1043	  have hcot := finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_algebraMap_residue hbij n hrank
1044	  -- (ii.B): dimension lower bound n ≤ dim Sₘ.
1045	  have hdim :=
1046	    Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.le_ringKrullDim_of_isLocalization_atPrime
1047	      (k := k) n m hm Sₘ
1048	  exact IsRegularLocalRing.of_finrank_cotangentSpace_le_ringKrullDim
1049	    (by rw [hcot]; exact_mod_cast hdim)
1050	
1051	/-- **Lane COE Step B.d′ — Stacks `00TT` at closed points over an algebraically
1052	closed field, unconditional form.** Over an algebraically closed base field the
1053	`k`-rationality hypothesis of Step B.d is automatic: for any maximal ideal
1054	`m ⊆ S` of the standard-smooth algebra `S`, the residue field `S ⧸ m` is a
1055	finite (Zariski's lemma, `finite_of_finite_type_of_isJacobsonRing`) hence
1056	algebraic extension of `k`, so `k → κ(m)` is bijective
1057	(`IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral` composed with
1058	`Ideal.bijective_algebraMap_quotient_residueField`). Hence the local ring of a
1059	standard-smooth `k̄`-algebra at **every** closed point is regular. Stated for
1060	the model localisation `Localization.AtPrime m`; transport to an abstract
1061	localisation (e.g. a scheme stalk) via `IsRegularLocalRing.of_ringEquiv` along
1062	`IsLocalization.algEquiv`. Axiom-clean. -/
1063	theorem isRegularLocalRing_localizationAtPrime_of_isStandardSmooth_of_isAlgClosed
1064	    {k : Type u} [Field k] [IsAlgClosed k]
1065	    {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Nontrivial S] [Algebra k S]
1066	    [Algebra.IsStandardSmooth k S]
1067	    (m : Ideal S) (hm : m.IsMaximal) :
1068	    IsRegularLocalRing (Localization.AtPrime m) := by
1069	  haveI := hm.isPrime
1070	  refine isRegularLocalRing_localization_of_isStandardSmooth_of_bijective_residue
1071	    (k := k) m hm (Localization.AtPrime m) ?_
1072	  -- Zariski's lemma: the residue field `S ⧸ m` is module-finite over `k`.
1073	  -- (The `Field` instance must be installed FIRST so all subsequent instance
1074	  -- searches on `S ⧸ m` share its semiring key.)
1075	  letI := Ideal.Quotient.field m
1076	  haveI : Algebra.FiniteType k (S ⧸ m) :=
1077	    Algebra.FiniteType.of_surjective (Ideal.Quotient.mkₐ k m) Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
1078	  haveI : Module.Finite k (S ⧸ m) := finite_of_finite_type_of_isJacobsonRing k (S ⧸ m)
1079	  haveI : Algebra.IsIntegral k (S ⧸ m) := Algebra.IsIntegral.of_finite k (S ⧸ m)
1080	  -- `k → S ⧸ m` is bijective since `k` is algebraically closed.
1081	  have h1 : Function.Bijective (algebraMap k (S ⧸ m)) :=
1082	    IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral
1083	  -- `S ⧸ m → κ(m)` is bijective since `m` is maximal.
1084	  have h2 := m.bijective_algebraMap_quotient_residueField
1085	  rw [show algebraMap k (IsLocalRing.ResidueField (Localization.AtPrime m))
1086	      = (algebraMap (S ⧸ m) m.ResidueField).comp (algebraMap k (S ⧸ m)) from
1087	    IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq k (S ⧸ m) m.ResidueField]
1088	  exact h2.comp h1
1089	
1090	/-! ## §3.C. Project-local Mathlib supplement — Matsumura regular-sequence bridge (iter-203, Step A1)
1091	
1092	Lane COE Step A1 substrate (Matsumura *Commutative Ring Theory* Thm 14.2 /
1093	Stacks 00NQ). The goal of this section is the criterion: on a regular local
1094	Noetherian ring `(A, 𝔪)`, a finite sequence `f₁,…,f_c ∈ 𝔪` whose images in the
1095	cotangent space `𝔪/𝔪²` are `κ`-linearly independent forms a
1096	`RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular` sequence.
1097	
1098	The induction is on the length `c`, peeling the head `f₁`. The mathematical
1099	peeling step uses `RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular.cons'`, whose tail lives over
1100	the *module* quotient `QuotSMulTop f₁ A = A ⧸ (f₁ • ⊤)`. The two bridges below
1101	convert that module-quotient bookkeeping into the *ring* quotient
1102	`A ⧸ Ideal.span {f₁}` (over which the induction hypothesis is naturally
1103	phrased): `quotSMulTop_quotientRing_linearEquiv` is the canonical
1104	`(A ⧸ span{f₁})`-linear identification `QuotSMulTop f₁ A ≃ₗ A ⧸ span{f₁}`, and
1105	`isRegular_cons_of_quotient_ring` is the resulting clean cons rule.
1106	
1107	These are project-local because Mathlib ships only the `QuotSMulTop`-flavoured
1108	`IsRegular.cons'`; the ring-quotient repackaging is what makes a downstream
1109	ring-theoretic induction (the Matsumura criterion) ergonomic. -/

## Review summary: Albanese T3 blueprint changes vs Lean

**Verdict: clean.** All six new `\leanok` nodes point at Lean declarations that exist under the fully-qualified names given, statements match, proofs are complete, the `\uses` DAG is correct and acyclic, and no node is marked green over a `sorry`.

### 1. Declaration existence + name/namespace + statement match

| Blueprint node | `\lean{...}` | Lean location | Match |
|---|---|---|---|
| `lem:mvPolynomial_height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal` | `MvPolynomial.height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal` | StandardSmoothDimension.lean:72 | ✓ `M.height = Nat.card ι` = "height exactly #I" |
| `lem:standard_smooth_le_height_of_isMaximal` | `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.natCast_le_height_of_isMaximal` | :126 (inside `namespace Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension`) | ✓ `(n:ℕ∞) ≤ m.height` |
| `lem:standard_smooth_le_ringKrullDim_localization` | `...le_ringKrullDim_of_isLocalization_atPrime` | :186 | ✓ `(n:WithBot ℕ∞) ≤ ringKrullDim Sₘ` with `[IsLocalization.AtPrime Sₘ m]` |
| `lem:regular_of_finrank_cotangent_le_dim` | `IsRegularLocalRing.of_finrank_cotangentSpace_le_ringKrullDim` | :208 | ✓ Noetherian local, `finrank κ (m/m²) ≤ ringKrullDim A ⟹ regular` |
| `lem:standard_smooth_regular_at_rational_point` | `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.isRegularLocalRing_localization_of_isStandardSmooth_of_bijective_residue` | CodimOneExtension.lean:1015 (inside `AlgebraicGeometry`→`Scheme`) | ✓ bijectivity is on `algebraMap k (ResidueField Sₘ)` = composite k→Sₘ→κ(m) |
| `lem:standard_smooth_regular_at_closed_point_algclosed` | `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.isRegularLocalRing_localizationAtPrime_of_isStandardSmooth_of_isAlgClosed` | :1063 | ✓ `[IsAlgClosed k]`, every maximal `m`, `IsRegularLocalRing (Localization.AtPrime m)` |

Namespace context verified: lines 1015/1063 sit inside `namespace AlgebraicGeometry` (123) → `namespace Scheme` (125), so the `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.` prefix in the blueprint is correct. Both are public `theorem`s (not `private`).

### 2. Proof completeness (house style)
All six blueprint proofs are complete rigorous arguments (induction over `#I`; submersive presentation + Krull height theorem; Nakayama + Krull; Zariski's lemma). No "clearly/omitted/TODO/sketch", no Lean tactics leaking into prose.

### 3. `\uses` edges — dependency-correct
- Every cited label resolves to a real node in the blueprint (all 7 sibling labels + `lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring` confirmed present, exactly one `\label` each).
- Node 5's 7 `\uses` each correspond to a real call in the Lean proof (lines 1031, 1034, 1035, 1037, 1043, 1046, 1048). Node 6 → node 5 matches the Lean call at line 1070. Node 3 → node 2 + `ringKrullDim_localization_eq_height_atPrime` matches lines 193–194. Node 2 → node 1 matches line 136.
- The extended `\uses` on `lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring` (line 1604) adds `lem:standard_smooth_regular_at_closed_point_algclosed`, a real sibling label (node 6). DAG is acyclic: `smooth_to_regular → node6 → node5 → {node3,node4,…} → node2 → node1`, all edges pointing to earlier lemmas.

### 4. No false green flags
- StandardSmoothDimension.lean: 0 `sorry`. CodimOneExtension.lean lines 1015–1089 (both new theorems): 0 `sorry`.
- `lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring`: its Lean `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` (CodimOneExtension.lean:1452, `private`) does still contain a `sorry` (line 1563), but the node's `\leanok` (line 1595) is **statement-level only** — it sits in the `lemma` environment; the `proof` environment (lines 1677–1712) carries **no** `\leanok`. That is honest per the convention (signature formalized, proof not). No violation.

### Minor observations (not defects)
- The six new proof environments carry no proof-level `\leanok`, even though their Lean proofs are complete and `sorry`-free. This under-claims (the DAG won't show them as fully proved) but is conservative/honest, not a false positive — worth adding proof `\leanok` in a later pass.
- Node 6's blueprint phrasing "the local ring S_m" is stated generically, while the Lean fixes the concrete model `Localization.AtPrime m`. Mathematically equivalent (transport via `IsRegularLocalRing.of_ringEquiv`, as the Lean docstring notes); a mild generality gap, not drift.
